word_list.word: the boughs that bear most hang lowest
word_list.pronunciation: ðə bˈaʊz ðæt bˈɛɹ mˈoʊst hˈæŋ lˈoʊəst

senses.definition: used to suggest that those who possess the greatest qualities, achievements, or responsibilities tend to be more humble and grounded

examples.x: The CEO of the company always gives credit to his team members and never takes all the glory for himself - the boughs that bear most hang lowest.

word_list.word: a loaded wagon makes no noise
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ lˈoʊdᵻd wˈæɡən mˌeɪks nˈoʊ nˈɔɪz

senses.definition: used to suggest that when someone is successful or accomplished, they often do not boast or draw attention to themselves

examples.x: Despite being one of the most accomplished musicians in the world, he never seeks attention or tries to show off his skills - a loaded wagon makes no noise.

word_list.word: the higher the monkey climbs, the more he shows his tail
word_list.pronunciation: ðə hˈaɪɚ ðə mˈʌnki klˈaɪmz ðə mˈoːɹ hiː ʃˈoʊz hɪz tˈeɪl

senses.definition: used to imply that as someone's status or position increases, their faults and shortcomings become more noticeable and harder to conceal
used to suggest that receiving recognition or honor can have an impact on one's behavior and attitude toward others, whether positive or negative

examples.x: The CEO's promotion to a higher position brought out a more arrogant side of him, demonstrating how honors change manners.

word_list.pronunciation: pɹˈaɪd kˈʌmz ɔːɹ ɡoʊz bɪfˌoːɹ ɐ fˈɔːl

senses.definition: used to imply that excessive pride or overconfidence can lead to a person's downfall or failure

examples.x: The CEO's pride and unwillingness to listen to feedback from his team members ended up costing the company millions of dollars, proving once again that pride comes before a fall.

senses.definition: used to imply that pride can cause a person to ignore or overlook discomfort or suffering, prioritizing maintaining their pride over resolving their problems or receiving help

examples.x: Despite the blisters on his feet, John refused to admit he couldn't finish the hike; pride feels no pain.

senses.definition: used to imply that when a person praises their own abilities or accomplishments, it is not a reliable or trustworthy recommendation compared to the opinions of others

examples.x: Despite the fact that the job applicant was highly qualified, his constant self-praise during the interview made the hiring manager question his suitability for the role, as self-praise is no recommendation.

senses.definition: used to highlight the negative impact of arrogance on a person's ability to gain and apply knowledge and wisdom

examples.x: Despite knowing that he lacked knowledge on the topic, Tom refused to listen to the opinions of experts, proving that arrogance diminishes wisdom.

word_list.word: if you have got it, flaunt it
word_list.pronunciation: ɪf juː hæv ɡˈɑːt ɪt flˈɔːnt ɪt

senses.definition: used to suggest that if one has a desirable quality or possession, it is acceptable to show it off, emphasizing the idea of being proud of one's achievements or possessions
